title: World Cup Train Tickets Cost Too Much
summary: The World Cup is coming to New Jersey, and fans might have to pay a lot of money to take the train to the games. Usually, a train ticket costs about $13, but for the World Cup, it could cost over $100. This is because of special event pricing. The governor of New Jersey, Mikie Sherrill, says FIFA, the group that runs the World Cup, should help pay for these train rides. She says FIFA is making billions of dollars from the World Cup, but they are not giving any money for transportation. This means fans, including kids and seniors, have to pay the full high price. The stadium in New Jersey will host eight matches, including the final. Governor Sherrill does not want regular train riders in New Jersey to pay for this cost for years. FIFA says they are surprised by her comments and think fans should pay only the normal cost.

keywords.explanation: A big soccer tournament where teams from around the world play.
A piece of paper or card that lets you ride on a train.
The group that organizes the World Cup and makes rules for soccer.
The leader of a state, like New Jersey.
When prices go up because of a big event like the World Cup.

questions.question: What is the usual cost of a train ticket to the stadium?
Who says FIFA should help pay for train rides?
How many matches will the stadium in New Jersey host?
What does FIFA say about the governor's comments?
Who has to pay the full fare for train tickets?
What is the name of the stadium during the World Cup?

background_read: The World Cup is a major international soccer event held every four years, attracting fans from all over the globe. In 2026, some matches will be in the United States, including in New Jersey. Transportation to stadiums can be expensive during big events because companies charge more due to high demand. FIFA is the organization that oversees soccer worldwide and earns money from ticket sales and sponsorships. Governors are elected officials who make decisions for their states, like New Jersey.
Article_Structure: WHO: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill and FIFA
WHAT: Train tickets to World Cup games might cost over $100 instead of the usual $13
WHERE: MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, which will host eight matches including the final
WHY: Because of special event pricing, and Governor Sherrill wants FIFA to help pay for transportation costs

perspectives.description: She believes FIFA should subsidize train fares since they are making a lot of money and fans shouldn't have to pay high prices.
